QF.id: IV Strategies Using Interest Rate Derivatives_8

question.text: A bond holder has a portfolio worth Rs. 25 crore with duration 5 years. Bond futures with duration 10 years are trading at Rs. 2,50,000 per contract. How many futures must be sold to reduce portfolio duration to 3 years?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Contracts needed = Portfolio value x (Target duration - Current duration) / (Futures price x Futures duration). = 25,00,00,000 x (3 - 5) / (2,50,000 x 10) = 25,00,00,000 x (-2) / 25,00,000 = -200. Negative means sell 200 contracts. **The Trap:** 500 uses full duration (5) instead of the change (2). 250 ignores futures duration. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is the modified hedge ratio for partial hedging, a more advanced version of the full hedge ratio in Q1. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** To reduce (not eliminate) duration, use the difference between target and current duration in the numerator.
